entries.description: Read dabbler-platform's upstream-feedback-disposition-gate.md (two issues: disposition gate undiscoverable, W0 runner SeededSignInAsync conflict). Read ai_router/disposition.py for Disposition dataclass + DISPOSITION_STATUSES ('completed', 'failed', 'requires_review') + VERIFICATION_METHODS ('api', 'queue'). Read Step 8 of docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md (lines 1099-1126). Read both error-message sites in ai_router/close_session.py (line 670-688 run_gate_checks disposition_present remediation, line 1304-1311 run() invalid_invocation). Read session_state.py for NextOrchestrator/NextOrchestratorReason/NEXT_ORCHESTRATOR_REASON_CODES. Confirmed no existing docs/disposition-schema.md.

Authored ai-assignment.md Session 1 portion with concrete edit plan (sections 2a-2g: schema doc, Step 8 edit, both error-message edits, test grep, smoke test, close-out artifacts), risk callouts, two open decisions presented to operator. Operator approved both recommendations: (1) parameterize <session-set-dir> with actual path rather than literal placeholder; (2) hold push until end of set; (3) defer --write-template flag.

Authored docs/disposition-schema.md (~265 lines): purpose statement, when-to-author (Step 8 of ai-led-session-workflow.md), file location convention, all 7 fields with type and required-conditions (status, summary, verification_method, files_changed, verification_message_ids, next_orchestrator, blockers), DISPOSITION_STATUSES values explained, VERIFICATION_METHODS values explained, NextOrchestrator shape with all 4 reason.code values, 3 gate-enforced invariants (method-vs-message-ids pairing, status=completed-not-final-session requires next_orchestrator, switch-due-to-blocker requires non-empty blockers), minimal viable template, 3 variation examples (outsource-last with queue ids, final session of set with next_orchestrator: null, switch-due-to-blocker pattern), '--force is not a substitute' section, cross-references to dataclass + close-out doc.

Edited docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md Step 8 (lines 1099-1126): added 'Authoring disposition.json' paragraph naming the file as a Step 8 author-deliverable before close_session runs, linked to docs/disposition-schema.md and to ai_router/disposition.py, flagged next_orchestrator + blockers as the two most-frequently-missed fields with their required-condition rules inline. Net delta: +14 lines, -1 line.

Edited ai_router/close_session.py at two error-message sites. Site 1 (run_gate_checks, line ~678): disposition_present remediation now uses an f-string with os.path.join(session_set_dir, 'disposition.json') for the actual path, then names required fields (status, summary, verification_method, files_changed, next_orchestrator-when-applicable, blockers-when-applicable), links to docs/disposition-schema.md and the Disposition dataclass, preserves force=True / incident-recovery / closeout_force_used clause. Site 2 (run(), line ~1307): invalid_invocation message mirrors the same path-and-fields-and-schema-link treatment, ends with pointer to close-out.md Section 5.

Ran python -m pytest ai_router/tests/ — 741 passed, 2 failed. The 2 failures (test_restart_role.py test_restart_signals_daemon_and_clears_pid_file + test_restart_orchestrator_daemon) are pre-existing Windows subprocess PID-mismatch flakes — confirmed unrelated by running pytest with my changes stashed (same 2 failures persisted). No regressions from Session 1's edits.

Created scratch directory at C:\Users\denmi\AppData\Local\Temp\sm-019 (empty, no disposition.json). Ran python -m ai_router.close_session --session-set-dir <scratch>. Output rendered the new invalid_invocation message: 'disposition.json is required at <path>. Required fields: status, summary, verification_method, files_changed, next_orchestrator (when status=completed and not the final session), blockers (when reason=switch-due-to-blocker). Schema: docs/disposition-schema.md...'. Confirmed parameterization works and schema-doc link is present.

Staged Session 1 files explicitly by name (not git add -A — avoids accidentally including the unrelated 0.13.2→0.13.3 tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package-lock.json drift from prior extension-publish work). Committed as 94260a6: 'Set 019 Session 1: disposition gate discoverability'. Not pushed per operator's hold-until-end-of-set call.

Read current UAT Checklist Rule + E2E Coverage Before UAT (workflow.md 657-740), Reading-Session-Set-Configuration prose (637-655), spec template (407-413), Rules list (1584-1640) confirming Rule 11 site, authoring guide config-block reference (132-196) and When-UAT-Is-Required (199-227), wizard tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/wizard/sessionGenPrompt.ts PROMPT_SYSTEM template, ai_router/docs/close-out.md Section 1 (disposition reference site pointing at the dead docs/session-state-schema-example.md).

Appended Session 2 plan to ai-assignment.md: 7 findings, 4 design decisions (default ad-hoc + skip transition heuristic, rule split shape, forward-compatible per-item fields, API single-verifier route at architecture complexity), 9 concrete edit sites (3a workflow.md template/prose; 3b UAT Rule restructure; 3c Choosing uatStyle; 3d Rule 11 split; 3e authoring guide; 3f wizard prompt; 3g upstream-feedback file; 3h close-out artifacts; 3i tests+commit+push+close-out), 3 open decisions (ProgrammaticVerification name vs alternatives, router-picks-verifier vs constrained, close-out.md back-link polish). Operator approved all three.

Four sequential edits to docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md. (1) Spec template added 'uatStyle: ad-hoc' line with comment. (2) §'Reading the Session Set Configuration' prose enumerates uatStyle alongside requiresUAT/requiresE2E/uatScope. (3) §'UAT Checklist Rule' rewritten into three subsections: shared preamble (filename, schema, editor URL, inline results, do-not-bypass-pending-review, opt-in-to-DSL note) + §'UAT Checklist Rule — DSL-driven (uatStyle=dsl)' (the former §'E2E Coverage Before UAT' content folded in, plus 'requires requiresE2E:true' callout) + §'UAT Checklist Rule — Ad-hoc (uatStyle=ad-hoc, the default)' (new — per-item ProgrammaticVerification or NoProgrammaticPathReason, with bUnit / SqlAssert / AI-exploratory examples; local gate, no uat-coverage-review route). §'When UAT Is Required' gained §'Choosing uatStyle' subsection mirroring the authoring-guide heuristic. (4) Rules list: Rule 11 split into 11a (DSL: Playwright parity + uat-coverage-review) + 11b (Ad-hoc: per-item ProgrammaticVerification/NoProgrammaticPathReason); existing Rule numbers preserved.

Two sequential edits to docs/planning/session-set-authoring-guide.md. (1) Config-block reference: added uatStyle line to YAML snippet; added 'uatStyle: dsl' and 'uatStyle: ad-hoc' field-semantics bullets between requiresE2E and uatScope; updated Defaults paragraph to include 'uatStyle: ad-hoc' as the default-when-omitted; added 'Migration note for dabbler-platform' callout describing the explicit declaration needed. (2) Added §'Choosing uatStyle' heuristic subsection after §'When UAT is required' — three bullets (dsl, ad-hoc, default-ad-hoc rationale + dabbler-platform exception) plus a Mixed Surfaces paragraph.

Edited tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/wizard/sessionGenPrompt.ts PROMPT_SYSTEM template: added 'uatStyle: <ad-hoc|dsl>' line to the generated spec template (positioned between requiresE2E and effort) and added a guideline bullet explaining when each value is appropriate. No VSIX rebuild needed — the prompt is read at clipboard-copy time, not compiled into runtime behavior.

Edited ai_router/docs/close-out.md Section 1 line 26: replaced the dead reference to docs/session-state-schema-example.md with a live link to docs/disposition-schema.md (the new Session 1 doc). One-line polish completing the cross-doc loop from operator's open-decisions list.

Authored docs/upstream-feedback/2026-05-11-w0-runner-pre-signin-conflict.md as a GitHub-issue-ready hand-off addressed to darndestdabbler/dabbler-uat-dsl. Restates the W0-runner SeededSignInAsync auto-injection conflict from the platform feedback file: what happens (cookie/login-page collision with TimeoutException on GetByLabel), why a consumer can't work around it, three concrete suggestions ordered by leverage (auto-detect /login as first step → skip pre-sign-in; --no-pre-sign-in CLI flag; PreSignIn:false checklist field), what the platform did in the meantime, cross-reference to Session 1's disposition fix, acceptance smoke test for the eventual fix.

Ran python -m pytest ai_router/tests/ --ignore=test_restart_role.py — 729 passed in 156s. No regressions from doc+config-only edits in Session 2. test_restart_role excluded because the 2 pre-existing PID-mismatch flakes from Session 1 are unrelated to this session's edits.

Routed end-of-session verification through ai_router: task_type=session-verification, complexity_hint=80, content=26605-char prompt with diff (20944 chars) + design summary + universal-core/gated-extensions philosophy quote from CLAUDE.md + 6 focus areas + structured JSON schema. Verifier auto-selected per cross-provider rule: GPT-5.4 (model_name=gpt-5-4, model_id=gpt-5.4, tier=3). 6937 input / 5728 output tokens. 87.6s elapsed. Cost $0.1033. Per memory feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling, RouteResult dumped to JSON before attribute access (c:\tmp\set019-session2-route-result.json). Verdict: ISSUES_FOUND with 2 issues — 1 Major (uatStyle=dsl + requiresE2E=false combination not explicitly invalid in docs) + 1 Minor (mixed-surfaces escape hatch incoherent for DSL mode).

Both issues addressed in-session. Major fix: added explicit 'Invalid combination' paragraph to workflow.md UAT Checklist Rule — DSL-driven subsection (rejected at Step 2, not silently downgraded — author must set requiresE2E:true OR switch to uatStyle:ad-hoc); mirrored extension on authoring-guide's uatStyle:dsl field-semantics bullet. Minor fix: rewrote mixed-surfaces paragraphs in both docs — combined web/non-web sets MUST use uatStyle:ad-hoc (the DSL path does not permit per-item exceptions); the prior 'use the other mode's tooling for the remainder' escape hatch was incoherent on DSL and has been removed.

Re-routed verification at complexity_hint=85 per Step 7 (Major issue triggers high effort). Same task type, same auto-selected GPT-5.4 verifier (cross-provider). Round 2 prompt (24879 chars) included Round 1 findings recap so the verifier could check resolution + look for newly-introduced issues. 96.3s elapsed. Cost $0.1098. Verdict: VERIFIED with empty issues list — Round 1 findings closed, no new issues introduced by the fixes. Cumulative Round 1+2 cost: $0.2131 (within $0.10-$0.25 projection).

Authored verification/gpt-5-4-round1.md and verification/gpt-5-4-round2.md (saved both rounds' content + metadata + cost). Authored change-log.md (narrative summary of both sessions, migration burden for dabbler-platform, deferred items list, verification trail, file lists per session, acceptance criteria checklist). Authored disposition.json (status:completed, verification_method:api, files_changed list of 15 files, verification_message_ids:[], next_orchestrator:null because Session 2 is the final session of Set 019, blockers:[]). Validated disposition.json against the Disposition dataclass with is_final_session=True — validation passed. Updated session-state.json to lifecycleState:closed, status:complete, completedAt timestamp. Authored this activity-log.json.

Committed Session 2 deliverables as f1001a7: 'Set 019 Session 2: uatStyle DSL/ad-hoc split + W0 upstream-feedback'. Pushed both 94260a6 (Session 1) + f1001a7 (Session 2) to origin/master per operator's hold-until-end-of-set call. Ran python -m ai_router.close_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/019-feedback-disposition-and-uat-two-options/ — dogfoods Session 1's disposition gate fix on Set 019 itself. First attempt caught a real defect: my activity-log.json used a sessions[].steps[] shape but the gate's check_activity_log_entry expected a flat top-level entries[] list. Rewrote activity-log.json to the canonical entries[] shape (this format), committed the fix, re-ran close_session.
